L1.area: Message filtering and selector processing layer

L2.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability allows remote consumers to have unspecified impact via vectors involving an XPath-based selector when dequeuing XML messages. The XML parser used for processing message content during selector evaluation does not disable external entity resolution, enabling attackers to craft malicious XML content that triggers external entity processing.

L3.area: XPath-based message selector evaluation within the message filtering subsystem
L3.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability exists in the XPath-based message selector evaluation logic in Apache ActiveMQ Apollo 1.x before 1.7.1. When dequeuing XML messages, the XML parser does not disable external entity resolution during XPath selector processing, allowing remote consumers to craft messages with external entity declarations that trigger XXE processing.

ground_truth.reason: The XML parser in the XPath-based message selector evaluation creates a DocumentBuilderFactory without disabling external entity resolution, allowing remote consumers to exploit XXE via crafted XML messages.
